a: Two of the following global string constants identify exceptions generated by the framework for Objective-C runtime errors and system exceptions such as invalid memory accesses. The other constant is used as a key to access the stack trace in the [userInfo](/documentation/Foundation/NSException/userInfo-swift.property) dictionary of an [NSException](/documentation/Foundation/NSException) object, when requested.
Monitor and debug exceptional conditions in code.

Use one or more of the following constants in the parameter of [setExceptionHandlingMask(_:)](/documentation/exceptionhandling/nsexceptionhandler/setexceptionhandlingmask(_:)) to specify the types of exceptions that the exception handler should monitor and whether it should handle or log them.
The following `#define` constants are conveniences for specifying complete sets of exception-handling `enum` constants.

The `NSExceptionHandler` class provides facilities for monitoring and debugging exceptional conditions in Objective-C programs. It works by installing a special uncaught exception handler via the  [NSSetUncaughtExceptionHandler(_:)](/documentation/Foundation/NSSetUncaughtExceptionHandler(_:)) function. Consequently, to use the services of `NSExceptionHandler`, you must not install your own custom uncaught exception handler.

The `NSExceptionHandlerDelegate` informal protocol describes methods that [NSExceptionHandler](/documentation/exceptionhandling/nsexceptionhandler) objects call on their delegates when exceptions occur. An [NSExceptionHandler](/documentation/exceptionhandling/nsexceptionhandler) object does not need to have a delegate. When one does, these delegate methods are asked to approve exception handling and logging for each monitored [NSExceptionHandler](/documentation/exceptionhandling/nsexceptionhandler) object.

The exception handler handles exceptions caught by handlers lower than the top-level handler by converting them to [NSException](/documentation/Foundation/NSException) objects containing a stack trace.
The exception handler handles exceptions caught by the top-level handler by converting them to [NSException](/documentation/Foundation/NSException) objects containing a stack trace.
The exception handler handles uncaught exceptions by terminating the thread in which they occur.
The exception handler handles uncaught runtime errors by converting them to [NSException](/documentation/Foundation/NSException) objects containing a stack trace.
The exception handler handles uncaught system exceptions by converting them to [NSException](/documentation/Foundation/NSException) objects containing a stack trace.
Combines via bitwise-OR all the constants listed in [System Hang Constants](/documentation/exceptionhandling/system-hang-constants).
The exception handler suspends execution when it detects an exception that would be handled by an object other than the top-level handler.
The exception handler suspends execution when it detects an exception that would be handled by the top-level handler.
The exception handler suspends execution when it detects an uncaught exception (other than a system exception or runtime error).
The exception handler suspends execution when it detects an uncaught runtime error.
The exception handler suspends execution when it detects an uncaught system exception.
Combines via bitwise-OR all the constants listed in [Logging and Handling Constants](/documentation/exceptionhandling/logging-and-handling-constants).
The exception handler logs exceptions caught by handlers lower than the top-level handler.
The exception handler logs exceptions that would be caught by the top-level handler.
The exception handler logs uncaught exceptions.
The exception handler logs uncaught runtime errors.
The exception handler logs uncaught system exceptions.

The key for fetching the stack trace (an [NSString](/documentation/Foundation/NSString) object) in the [userInfo](/documentation/Foundation/NSException/userInfo-swift.property) dictionary of the [NSException](/documentation/Foundation/NSException) object passed into one of the delegate methods described in [NSExceptionHandlerDelegate](/documentation/ExceptionHandling/nsexceptionhandlerdelegate#Logging-and-handling-exceptions).
Identifies an Objective-C runtime error.
Identifies an uncaught system exception.

Use one or more of the following constants in the parameter of [setExceptionHangingMask(_:)](/documentation/exceptionhandling/nsexceptionhandler/setexceptionhangingmask(_:)) to specify the types of exceptions that cause the exception to halt execution so a debugger can be attached.
Returns the singleton `NSExceptionHandler` instance.
Returns the delegate of the `NSExceptionHandler` object.
Returns a bit mask representing the types of exceptions monitored by the receiver and its handling and logging behavior.
Returns a bit mask representing the types of exceptions that will halt execution for debugging.
Sets the delegate of the `NSExceptionHandler` object.
Sets the bit mask of constants specifying the types of exceptions monitored by the receiver and its handling and logging behavior.
Sets the bit mask of constants specifying the types of exceptions that will halt execution for debugging.
